item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If anyone asked, we said we threw the thing way.

Is "way" a typo for "away" or is this an attempt at establishing some sort of accent or speech pattern? It doesn't seem consistent with the rest of the interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glory be to our blessed Greenspan. Peace be upon our stocks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea is the right kind of silly, the article is well written, and that interview is terrific. Great work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interview is what really makes this one for me. That and the fact that this is a shrine to Alan Greenspan. The guy was a groupie (and alleged lover) of Ayn Rand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love bonkers ritual stuff. This really hits the right notes, from the bizarro prayer, the list of offerings and the interview tying everything together. It teeters on the edge of not being possible to take seriously but it works for me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
However, all anomalous properties cease if the picture of Alan Greenspan is replaced with a photograph of Ben Bernanke or other Federal Reserve chairs such as Janet Yellen or Paul Volcker.

This line stood out to me. Are the names of other Federal Reserve chairs important or could you simply say “anomalous properties cease to function if the image is replaced by other Federal Reserve chair members.”

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was kinda 'meh' on the interview (interviews are a bit overdone at this point), but for some reason I didn't see those last few lines coming and something about the deadpan delivery sold it. +1, not perfect, but I enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this one. The prayer was the best part for me.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There was one error I spotted, if it even is on. It’s in Appendix B, it has an error with saying ‘SCP-XXXX’. Though it is not to my knowledge if it was to be left in as I read it over multiple times, still a +1 mate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like the mention of him not having thumbs is a bit too blunt, it'd be a lot better if you cut that part and just trusted the reader to make the connection when he was talking about a 10% rise - but I understand if you feel like that'd be too weird for an interviewer to overlook.

Aside from that, I can't help but feel like the interview format is kinda getting passé, but I don't know how you'd tell this story otherwise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the sort of realistic yet wacky magic skip I like, and to top it off it's written well and the dialogue is snappy. Easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ooh, I really enjoyed this. Fairly good topic and realistic dialogue. However I do feel things like curse words and maybe(?) the brand name of the pens.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The image is in the public domain: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alan_Greenspan#/media/File:Alan_Greenspan_color_photo_portrait.jpg

This is based on a minor hoax that took place in the late 90's (see the third paragraph here: https://www.theglobalist.com/the-second-coming-of-saint-paul/ )

Thanks to Zyn for giving me feedback in the drafts forum.
